title: Enhance Combined %, Nine Grading, Subject Analysis, and Certificate features

requirements.summary: Update Combined Percentage view to compute overall percentage per grade by averaging adjusted percentages across all available terms, dividing by 2 for two terms or 3 for three terms
acceptanceCriteria: When data for two terms exists for a grade, the overall percentage equals (Term1 adjusted % + Term2 adjusted %) / 2.
When data for three terms exists for a grade, the overall percentage equals (Term1 + Term2 + Term3 adjusted %) / 3.
Overall percentage is displayed per grade in the Combined Percentage view.
The division denominator is determined dynamically by the number of distinct terms present in the data for that grade.

file_operations.description: Update the Combined Percentage view to compute and display an 'Overall %' column per grade. For each grade grouping, calculate the overall percentage by summing all adjusted term percentages and dividing by the count of distinct terms (2 or 3). The denominator must be dynamically determined based on how many terms are present for that grade. Display the result prominently as a single overall percentage value per grade row or card.

Add a helper function to compute overall percentage per grade by accepting an array of adjusted term percentages and returning their average: sum all provided percentages and divide by the array length (which will be 2 or 3). This function should handle dynamic term counts without hardcoding divisors.

requirements.summary: Update Nine Scale Grades view to display a 9-point scale grade for each subject, for each term, for each grade level using the scale 90–100→9, 80–89→8, 70–79→7, 60–69→6, 50–59→5, 40–49→4, 30–39→3, 20–29→2, 0–19→1
acceptanceCriteria: Each row or card in the Nine Scale view shows the student's grade level, term, and the 9-point scale grade for every subject.
Data is grouped or filterable by grade level and term.
Every subject present for that grade/term combination shows a distinct 9-point scale badge.

file_operations.description: Update the Nine Scale Grades view to display a 9-point scale grade (1–9) for every subject in every term of every grade level. For each academic entry, compute the 9-scale grade for each subject using its raw marks and the grading scale: 90–100=9, 80–89=8, 70–79=7, 60–69=6, 50–59=5, 40–49=4, 30–39=3, 20–29=2, 0–19=1. Display each subject's 9-scale grade as a distinct badge. Ensure the view supports filtering by grade level and term, and that data is organized so users can see per-subject grades for each grade/term combination.

Add or update a function to convert a raw percentage (0–100) into a 9-point scale grade using the exact boundaries: 90–100→9, 80–89→8, 70–79→7, 60–69→6, 50–59→5, 40–49→4, 30–39→3, 20–29→2, 0–19→1. This function should accept a numeric percentage and return an integer between 1 and 9. Use this helper in the NineScaleGradesView to compute per-subject grades from raw marks.

requirements.summary: Enhance Subject Analysis view to show average raw marks, average percentage, highest raw marks with percentage, and lowest raw marks with percentage for each subject, with grade and term filter controls
acceptanceCriteria: Each subject card/row displays: average raw marks (numeric), average percentage, highest raw marks with its percentage, and lowest raw marks with its percentage.
A grade filter dropdown narrows analysis to entries for the selected grade.
A term filter dropdown narrows analysis to entries for the selected term.
Both filters can be applied simultaneously.
Statistics update reactively when filters change.
When no filter is applied, statistics are computed across all grades and all terms.

file_operations.description: Enhance the Subject Analysis view to display four statistics per subject: (1) average raw marks (numeric), (2) average percentage, (3) highest raw marks with its corresponding percentage, and (4) lowest raw marks with its corresponding percentage. Add two filter dropdowns: one for grade and one for term. When filters are applied, recompute statistics only for entries matching the selected grade and/or term. When no filters are applied, compute statistics across all grades and all terms. Ensure that both filters can be applied simultaneously and that statistics update reactively when filters change.

Update the subject analysis computation logic to support optional grade and term filters. Modify existing functions to accept optional grade and term parameters and filter the academic entries before computing statistics. Ensure that for each subject, the library computes: average raw marks (numeric mean), average percentage, highest raw marks with its percentage, and lowest raw marks with its percentage. When no filters are provided, compute across all entries.

requirements.summary: Add or update Certificate section to generate a certificate for each grade and term, showing a computed percentage of (original term percentage + 94) / 2, with grade and term filter controls
acceptanceCriteria: A certificate is generated for every grade–term combination that has data.
The percentage shown on the certificate equals (original term percentage + 94) / 2.
A grade filter dropdown limits displayed certificates to the selected grade.
A term filter dropdown limits displayed certificates to the selected term.
Both filters can be applied simultaneously.
The certificate displays the student's grade level, term, and the computed certificate percentage clearly.

file_operations.description: Update the Certificate view to generate a certificate for every grade–term combination present in the academic data. For each certificate, compute the displayed percentage using the formula: (original term percentage + 94) / 2. Add two filter dropdowns: one for grade and one for term. When filters are applied, display only certificates matching the selected grade and/or term. Both filters can be applied simultaneously. Ensure each certificate clearly displays the student's grade level, term, and the computed certificate percentage. Wire the generated certificate images from frontend/public/assets/generated/ (certificate-platinum.dim_400x250.png, certificate-gold.dim_400x250.png, certificate-silver.dim_400x250.png, certificate-bronze.dim_400x250.png) into the UI as static assets, selecting the appropriate tier based on the computed certificate percentage.
